Japanese: 五十嵐さん。最初に言われたこと、 おぼえていますね？
English: Mr. Kudo. Do you remember what you were told at the start of this testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥この証言は、あなたの記憶力を 試すためのものであること‥‥
English: That this was a way of testing the credibility of your memory?

Japanese: わかっとる！　今度こそ、 マチガイない！　ゼーッタイにな！
English: I know, I know! There's nothing wrong with my memory, I tell you! Nothing!

Japanese: まちがっとったら、マメ食いながら “はとぽっぽ”歌ってやるワ！
English: If I got anything wrong, I'll eat these seeds and sing the pigeon song!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコトだ？ まるほどう。
English: Care to tell us where this is going, Trite?

Japanese: 被害者は、右手で新聞をめくり、 あいている手でコーヒーを飲んだ。
English: According to Mr. Kudo, the victim was holding the paper in his right hand...

Japanese: ‥‥つまり、カップを持ったのは “左手”ということになります。
English: ...while drinking coffee with his free hand, which would make that his left.

Japanese: フン！　それぐらいの引き算は できるみたいだの！
English: Kah! What is this? Kindergarten?

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: But I would like the court to please take a look at this.

Japanese: それは‥‥被害者が 使っていたものですな？
English: That's the cup the victim used, correct?

Japanese: カップのフチに、コーヒーを 飲んだ跡が残っています。
English: Yes, and on the rim, you'll notice the mark left by the victim's lips.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥残っていますね。 口をつけた跡が。
English: Yes. There is a stain left by the coffee.

Japanese: ‥‥その跡を見れば、 ハッキリわかるのです。
English: If you consider where that stain is, you'll clearly see...

Japanese: 被害者が、右手で コーヒーを飲んだことがッ！
English: ...that the victim was holding the cup in his right hand!

Japanese: あれ‥‥
English: But how...?

Japanese: それでは、五十嵐さん‥‥ はりきって、どうぞ！
English: Well, Mr. Kudo. The court is waiting for your epic performance!

Japanese: マメを食べながら“はとぽっぽ”を 歌っていただきましょうか！
English: You said you'd eat those seeds and sing the pigeon song!

Japanese: ぐむむむううぅぅッ！
English: Aaaargh!

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。 いたしかたありませんな。
English: Mr. Kudo... I'm afraid this is simply not acceptable.

Japanese: ‥‥この証人には、公園に帰って もらったほうがいいようですな
English: I think the witness had better go back to the park where he came from.

Japanese: ちょっと待てええええぇぇぇぇぃ！
English: WAAAAIT!

Japanese: だまって聞いておれば‥‥ いい気になりおって！
English: If you think I'm going to stand here and listen to you tell me I'm mad, you're wrong!

Japanese: 湯飲みのヨゴレがなんだ！ ワシはゼッタイ、まちがえとらん！
English: I don't care about that dirty coffee cup! I know what I saw!

Japanese: ま、まだ言いますか‥‥
English: Y-You still insist on your testimony?

Japanese: あのワカゾウは、たしかに 左手でコーシーを飲んでおった！
English: That young brat was holding the cup in his left hand!

Japanese: そうなのだ！　ワシはゼーンゼン、 悪くないもんね！
English: Oh, yes. No question! I'm a good, law-abiding citizen, I am.

Japanese: 悪いのは、あの死んだワカゾウと、 そこのアタマのトガったガキだッ！
English: It's that dead young hotbot and you, you spikey-haired yahoo, who are at fault!

Japanese: （ぼくかよ！）
English: (Who, me!?)

Japanese: いやいや、おジイさん。 もうケッコウですから‥‥
English: Thank you, old man. We've heard quite enough from you already.

Japanese: アンタに“おジイさん”呼ばわり されたくないワ！
English: Don't call me "old man", old man!

Japanese: たのむ！　ワシの６８年の人生が、 完全否定されようとしとるんだよ！
English: Been around for 68 years, I have. You can't ignore me!

Japanese: ワシの言うコトを 聞いてくだされえええぇぇッ！
English: LISTEN TO WHAT I'VE GOT TO SAAAAY!

Japanese: ザンネンですが、証人‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Kudo, but...

Japanese: ‥‥いいだろうぜ。
English: Sure, why not hear a little more?

Japanese: ご‥‥‥ゴドー検事！
English: M-Mr. Godot!

Japanese: ただし‥‥‥このコーヒーは １６杯めだ。
English: But this is my 16th cup of coffee...

Japanese: 次は、ねえぜ。
English: So this is your final stand.

Japanese: ありがたい、大将！　この将兵、 見かけよりも、やりますぞッ！
English: Thank you, Captain! You can rely on Victor!

Japanese: ～右手？　左手？～
English: -- Left Hand or Right Hand --

Japanese: ヤツは、メガネをかけたほうの耳に ラジオのイヤホンをハメとった！
English: The boy was wearing the earpiece on the same side as the green lens of his specs.

Japanese: そして、そのイヤホンを、しきりに 手でいじっとったのですワ！
English: He kept fiddling with it all the time.

Japanese: コーシーを飲む前にも、ヤツは イヤホンをいじった！
English: He was fiddling with it just before he picked up the cup, too.

Japanese: そしてそのまま、その手で コーシーを飲んだ。左手でなッ！
English: And then he used the same hand to pick up the cup. His left hand!

Japanese: たしかに、被害者は左目に、 フシギなメガネをかけていますな。
English: We know that the victim was wearing an unusual monocle over his left eye.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに、そいつは コワれたメガネじゃねえ。
English: It wasn't a monocle, Your Honor.

Japanese: コンピュータ・プログラマーが 使う、小型のモニターだぜ。
English: It was a small computer monitor often used by programmers.

Japanese: “もにたー”？　‥‥テレビジョン のようなものですか？
English: A monitor? You mean, like a television screen!?

Japanese: そのレンズの内側に、コンピュータ の情報が表示されるらしい。
English: The inside of the lens is a screen that displays computer data.

Japanese: 《モノクル》と呼ばれる、 彼らのシゴト道具だ。
English: It's called an HMD. It's a common tool in the victim's line of work.

Japanese: モノクロでもカラーでもいいが、 これでわかっただろ！
English: HDTV, DVD, CD... All these newfangled letters drive me mad! But they don't matter.

Japanese: ワシが、ハッキリ自信を持って しゃべっとるコトが！
English: I know what I saw, and I'm telling the truth!

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ウソをついている ようすは、まったくない）
English: (It's true... He doesn't seem to be lying.)

Japanese: さァ！　白黒ハッキリ してもらおうじゃねえかッ！
English: And those are the facts, in good old black and white!

Japanese: ～右手？　左手？～
English: -- Left Hand or Right Hand --

Japanese: ヤツは、メガネをかけたほうの耳に ラジオのイヤホンをハメとった！
English: The boy was wearing the earpiece on the same side as the green lens of his specs.

Japanese: そして、そのイヤホンを、しきりに 手でいじっとったのですワ！
English: He kept fiddling with it all the time.

Japanese: コーシーを飲む前にも、ヤツは イヤホンをいじった！
English: He was fiddling with it just before he picked up the cup, too.

Japanese: そしてそのまま、その手で コーシーを飲んだ。左手でなッ！
English: And then he used the same hand to pick up the cup. His left hand!

Japanese: どう？　なるほどくん。
English: Well, Nick? What do you think?

Japanese: イガラシさん‥‥ホントのコトを 証言しているとしか思えない。
English: I think the guy's telling the truth.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥
English: But even so...

Japanese: どう考えても、おかしい点がある。
English: Something's not quite right.

Japanese: ホント！　じゃ、じゃあ、 たたきつけてやろうよ、証拠をさ！
English: Then chuck evidence at him until he breaks!

Japanese: （でも‥‥ウソをついてないのに、 なぜ、ムジュンするんだ‥‥？）
English: (But if he's not lying, there wouldn't be any contradictions in his testimony, right?)

Japanese: 《モノクル》ですか‥‥
English: So the victim was wearing an HMD.

Japanese: モノクロでもカラーでも どっちでもいいワ！
English: HDTV, CD, DVD... What does it matter!?

Japanese: （モノクロでもカラーでも ないけどな‥‥）
English: (It was none of them actually, but anyway...)

Japanese: そっちの耳にイヤホンを つけていたのは、たしかですか？
English: And you're sure that he was wearing the earpiece on the same side?

Japanese: ワシのいたところからは、 そっちの耳しか見えんかった！
English: No question! I could only see that side of his head from where I was sitting.

Japanese: （上面図で考えれば、 当然だな‥‥）
English: (Yeah. That's pretty obvious if you look at the floor plans.)

Japanese: （向かい側のテーブルからは、 被害者の横顔しか見えない‥‥）
English: (From the opposite table, he'd only have a view of the side of the victim's head.)

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんのこと、 よく見ていますね‥‥
English: It seems you kept an eye on Mr. Glen Elg.

Japanese: とにかく、気にさわったのだ！
English: He was getting on my nerves!

Japanese: 新聞はパサパサめくりたおすワ、 イヤホンはイジりたおすワ！
English: Rustling that newspaper and fiddling with his earpiece all the time!

Japanese: あげくの果てに、コーシー ヒトクチでバッタリ死ぬワ！
English: And then he went and made all that fuss dying from one sip of javaccino!

Japanese: “もっと落ちつけ！” と、そう言いたいッ！
English: I wanted to say to him, "Calm down, you young brat!"

Japanese: 見ていたワシのマメ食うペースも、 ミョーに早くなっちまった！
English: Just looking at him made me suddenly speed up my seed eating! I could've choked!

Japanese: （とにかく、目だつ客だった みたいだな‥‥被害者）
English: (So I take it the victim was a walking ball of nervous energy...)

Japanese: そのイヤホン‥‥ どっちの手でいじっていましたか？
English: The earpiece you mentioned... Which hand did the victim touch it with?

Japanese: アンタ、アレか？
English: You're one of those people!

Japanese: 左のポケットに入ってるマメを、 わざわざ右手で取り出すのか？
English: One of those people who uses his left hand to get things out of his right pocket.

Japanese: シャツの左のソデに、 わざわざ右ウデを通すのか？
English: Or fastens his left cuff with his right hand?

Japanese: バスガイドさんに『右手を ごらんください』と言われて‥‥
English: And when the tour guide says, "On your right side, you'll see the famous blah-blah"...

Japanese: アンタだけ、 わざわざ左を見るのかッ！
English: ...you're the only one who deliberately looks left! Well? Aren't you!?

Japanese: そ、そういうイミでは‥‥
English: N-No! I didn't mean...

Japanese: イヤホンをハメとった側の 手に決まってるだろうがッ！
English: Obviously he used the hand on the same side of his body that the earpiece was in!

Japanese: いてててて！
English: Ow! Oooow! Ow!

Japanese: （つまり、《モノクル》をつけた ほうの手‥‥左手か‥‥）
English: (So, if he had the HMD on his left side, then it was his left hand, I guess...)

Japanese: 自信たっぷり、ですね。
English: You seem very sure of yourself, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: だってワシ、見たからの！ アンタがなんと言おうと！
English: That's because I know what I saw, no matter what tricks you try to play on me!

Japanese: （たぶん、ホントに 見たんだろうな‥‥）
English: (It looks like he really did see the guy picking the cup up with his left hand.)

Japanese: （‥‥さすがに、 つっこみようがないもんな）
English: (This is a dead end!)

Japanese: ‥‥このムジュンが何を イミするのかわかりませんが‥‥
English: I'm not sure what the relevance of this is, but...

Japanese: 証人！　あなたが目撃した被害者 ‥‥決定的に不自然な点がある！
English: Mr. Kudo! There is something very strange about your observations of the victim.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: 《モノクル》をつけたほうの耳に イヤホンをつけていたそうですね。
English: You say he was wearing the earpiece on the same side as the HMD.

Japanese: まちがいないぞ！　ブチこんで やがったのさ。左の耳にな！
English: No question! You can lock me up if I'm wrong! It was his left ear, without a doubt!

Japanese: ワシのテーブルからは、そっちの 耳しか見えなかったんだ！
English: I could only see that side of his head from where I was sitting.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなハズはないんですよ。
English: I don't think so.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトだ‥‥？
English: Wh-What did you say!?

Japanese: あなたは、ご存じ ないでしょうね‥‥証人。
English: You're no doubt unaware of this fact, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: 被害者の左耳は‥‥、 コマクが破れていたんですよ！
English: But the victim couldn't hear with his left ear! His eardrum was ruptured!
Eh?

Japanese: 耳には、クスリを 使用した跡も残っています！
English: Traces of medication for his condition were found in his ear canal.

Japanese: そう！　被害者が イヤホンをつけるハズがない！
English: That's right! It's impossible that the victim was wearing his earpiece in his left ear.

Japanese: その耳は、 聞こえなかったのだから！
English: Because he couldn't even hear in that ear!

Japanese: ホントなのか？　大将‥‥
English: Is that true, Captain?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかだ。
English: ...It is.

Japanese: ぽっぽっぽお！ ハトぽっぽお‥‥かぁ！
English: Pi-Pi-Pigeon! Hm-hm-hmm... Pretty pigeon! Hm-hm-hmm...

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: この証人の証言は、 メチャクチャです！
English: This witness's testimony is completely unreliable!

Japanese: カンジンのウエイトレスは、 その後ろ姿しか見ていない！
English: He only saw the waitress from behind.

Japanese: そして、被害者は、聞こえない 耳にイヤホンをしていたと言う！
English: And he claims the victim was wearing an earpiece when we know his eardrum was ruptured!

Japanese: いかがですか！　ゴドー検事！
English: Well, Mr. Godot!?
Aaaargh!

Japanese: カップの中の闇をニゴらせるには、 ミルクを一滴、たらせばいい。
English: A single drop of milk is all it takes to destroy the pure black magic in the cup!

Japanese: このジイさん‥‥ そのミルクだったようだぜ。
English: This old man... ...is my drop of milk!

Japanese: 大将！　ヒトをシズクみたいに 言わないでいただきたい‥‥
English: Captain! Are you calling me a drip!?

Japanese: ここに、青酸カリが検出された コーヒーカップがあります。
English: This is the victim's coffee cup in which the potassium cyanide was found.

Japanese: フチのよごれから、右手で 使用されたのは、アキラカです！
English: The mark on the rim clearly shows that the victim picked it up with his right hand!

Japanese: ワシは死ぬまで 叫びつづけてやるぞ！
English: I'll never back down! I know I'm right!

Japanese: ヤツは死ぬ前、たしかに 左手でコーシーを飲んだんだ！
English: The lad drank his javaccino with his left hand!

Japanese: おそらく‥‥被害者は、 その両方をやったのさ。
English: Let me put you out of your misery... Clearly, the victim used both hands.

Japanese: 右手でコーヒーを飲んだあと、 左手にカップを持ちかえた‥‥
English: He took a sip with the cup held in his right hand, and then switched to his left...

Japanese: ジイさんは、それを目撃したんだ！
English: That's what the old man saw!

Japanese: それは、あり得ません！
English: Impossible!

Japanese: 先ほどから、何度も 証言されているはずです！
English: The witness has already testified on numerous occasions...

Japanese: 被害者は、コーヒーをヒトクチ 飲んだ直後に死んだ、と！
English: ...that the victim died immediately after taking just one sip of the coffee.

Japanese: コーヒーを飲んだ手‥‥そんなの、 どっちだって同じコトだぜ！
English: Which hand the victim used to pick up his cup is irrelevant, Your Honor.

Japanese: ダイジなのは‥‥ヤツがコーヒーを 飲んだコトだけだ！
English: The facts still stand! With one hand or the other, Mr. Elg drank the poisoned coffee...

Japanese: ‥‥こんなふうにッ！
English: ...Like this!

Japanese: ザンネンながら、 そうは行きません！
English: Sadly, Mr. Godot, that doesn't wash!

Japanese: この証言の目的は、証人の 記憶力をテストすることでした！
English: The point of this testimony was to establish whether the witness's memory was credible.

Japanese: 結果はアキラカです！
English: And the results are clear!

Japanese: この証人の証言は、 まったくアテにならないッ！
English: The testimony given by this witness is useless!

Japanese: ぶふおおオオォォッ！
English: Puuuurgh!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥本日の審理は ここまでのようですな。
English: I believe it is time to conclude today's proceedings.

Japanese: 証人は、たしかにウソは ついていないようですが‥‥
English: I am satisfied that the witness is not deceiving the court.

Japanese: その証言は、ハッキリ言って あまりにもムチャクチャです。
English: But to be frank, his testimony is a farce!

Japanese: そそ。そんな言い方しなくても いいではないかッ！
English: D-Did you have to be so frank!?

Japanese: 食らえッ！　このッ！
English: Take that, you pompous old fogey!

Japanese: ‥‥ムダムダ。 ここまでは届きません。
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Kudo. You can't reach me from there.

Japanese: 検察側、弁護側に、 さらなる調査を命じます。
English: I'm ordering the defense and the prosecution to investigate this case further.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは！ 本日は、これにて閉廷！
English: That is all for now. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: ちょっと待っていただきたい！
English: WAAAAIT!

Japanese: このまま終わったら、ワシの立場は いったい、どうなるのか！
English: If we stop now, where does that leave me?

Japanese: ‥‥‥たちば？
English: Leave you, Mr. Kudo?

Japanese: そこの青いワカゾウのおかげで、 今や、このワシは‥‥
English: Thanks to that blue-suited young upstart over there...

Japanese: ただの、スットコチョイで オッチョコドッコイのジジイだ！
English: ...I'm just a bumbling old man who can't even dot his t's or cross his i's now!

Japanese: （ぼくのせいかよ‥‥）
English: (How is your bad memory my fault!?)

Japanese: そ、そう言われましても‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Kudo, but there's nothing I can do.

Japanese: ジツは‥‥ワケあって ダマっておったのだが‥‥
English: I've kept my mouth shut until now...

Japanese: ワシにはまだ、 言ってないことがあるッ！
English: ...but there's something else the court should know!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと！
English: What!?

Japanese: “言ってないこと”‥‥！
English: There's more?

Japanese: ‥‥ショージキなトコロ、 あんまり大したことではない。
English: To be perfectly honest, it might not be anything.

Japanese: しかし！　それでもう一度だけ、 勝負がしたい！　キサマとッ！
English: But I want another chance! I want another crack at you, you young shark!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥ （目のカタキにされてるな‥‥）
English: M-Me? (He's looking at me like I'm some sort of evil Shogun...)

Japanese: どうかみなさん、 つきあってもらえまいか！
English: Well, everyone? What do you say to one final showdown?

Japanese: この、ヘンクツオヤジの 思い出づくりに！
English: The final chapter in this eccentric old man's scrapbook!

Japanese: ワルいが‥‥
English: Sorry, gramps...

Japanese: １７杯目のコーヒーはもう、 飲み終えちまったぜ。
English: I've already had my 17th cup of coffee.

Japanese: いいではないか、大将！
English: What have you got to lose, Captain?

Japanese: あとで、ワシの店に来なさい！ あびるほど飲ませてやりますぞ！
English: I'll give you all the coffee you want if you come to my house after the trial.

Japanese: この五十嵐　将兵、６８才。 オトコにしてくだされえッ！
English: I may be 68 years old, but Victor Kudo is still a man!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: That's enough, witness.

Japanese: こうなったら、 話を聞いたほうが早いでしょう。
English: I believe it will be quicker for the court to just hear your testimony.

Japanese: あらよッと！ そう来なくっちゃ！
English: You bet! Much, much quicker!

Japanese: スゴいことになっちゃったね‥‥
English: I can't believe this is happening...

Japanese: カッカッカッ！ カクゴしろよ、このワカゾウが！
English: Ha ha ha! You better get ready, youngster!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりましたから、 マメを投げないでください‥‥。
English: I get the picture! Just quit throwing those seeds at me, would you!?

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥まだ、 やられ足りないのか‥‥）
English: (He's gotta be using some sort of infinite ammo code with that box of seeds...)

Japanese: ～最後の大勝負～
English: -- The Final Showdown --

Japanese: 最初にくれぐれも言っておく。 大したコトでもなく、自信もない！
English: First of all, I want to stress that this might be nothing. I'm not too sure of myself.

Japanese: コーシーを飲んだ瞬間、ワカゾウは 机につっぷしたのだが‥‥
English: The young boy slumped over the table as soon as he took one sip of his javaccino...

Japanese: ヤッコさん、ハデにひっくり返した のだ！　その‥‥花ビンをな！
English: Well, the clumsy idiot upset the vase! He knocked it right over.

Japanese: 花ビンは割れて、机にかかっていた 布キレも、ビショビショになった！
English: It broke, and the strip of cloth covering the table got completely soaked!

Japanese: ‥‥どうだ！　さすがにコレは ひっくり返せまいて！
English: Well!? How about that? Turned things upside-down, hmm?

Japanese: あの。それだけですか？
English: Um... Is that all?

Japanese: そうだが。 ワシの大切な思い出だよ。
English: Yes. That's all. I remember it perfectly.

Japanese: なんだ！　また疑っとるのか！ このミジメでアワレなワシを！
English: Eh!? You're doubting me again? You're doubting a poor, defenseless old man!?

Japanese: い、いえ。そういう ワケではないのですが‥‥
English: N-No. We are not doubting you, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: なるほどくん。法廷中のヒトが 何か言いたそうだよ。
English: Don't you get the feeling there's a question hanging on everyone's lips, Nick?

Japanese: たぶん‥‥、『それがどうした』 だろうね。
English: Yeah. "So what?" probably!

Japanese: ‥‥この証言に尋問するぼくが、 イチバン言いたいコトバだ。
English: That's all I can think of, and I have to cross-examine this guy!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いわゆる マメ知識というヤツですな！
English: You're a birdbrain, that's why that's all you can think of!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。 最後の尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well... Mr. Wright. Your final cross- examination, please.

Japanese: ～最後の大勝負～
English: -- The Final Showdown --

Japanese: 最初にくれぐれも言っておく。 大したコトでもなく、自信もない！
English: First of all, I want to stress that this might be nothing. I'm not too sure of myself.

Japanese: コーシーを飲んだ瞬間、ワカゾウは 机につっぷしたのだが‥‥
English: The young boy slumped over the table as soon as he took one sip of his javaccino...

Japanese: ヤッコさん、ハデにひっくり返した のだ！　その‥‥花ビンをな！
English: Well, the clumsy idiot upset the vase! He knocked it right over.

Japanese: 花ビンは割れて、机にかかっていた 布キレも、ビショビショになった！
English: It broke, and the strip of cloth covering the table got completely soaked!

Japanese: ‥‥どうだ！　さすがにコレは ひっくり返せまいて！
English: Well!? How about that? Turned things upside-down, hmm?

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん、ギラギラした目で ニラんでるな。真宵ちゃんを。
English: He's really giving you the evil eye, Maya.

Japanese: なるほどくんでしょ！
English: It's you he's looking at, Nick, not me!

Japanese: “ムジュンがあるなら、 言ってみやがれ”って感じだね。
English: It's like he's saying, "I triple dog dare you to find a contradiction, young'un!"

Japanese: しょうがないな。 手みじかに言ってやるか‥‥。
English: I guess I'll just have to rise to the challenge then...

Japanese: あの‥‥できれば、自信があって ゆさぶりがいのある証言を‥‥
English: Um... The court generally prefers if witnesses are sure of themselves, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: うるさいわッ！
English: Noisy brat!

Japanese: 今、ワシは手負いのイノシシより タケり狂っとるのだ！
English: I'm warning you, I'm more savage than a macho man right now!

Japanese: この証言で、 アンタと最後の大勝負だッ！
English: You won't beat me with this testimony! This is the final battle!

Japanese: いてててて！
English: Ow! Oooow!

Japanese: （マメのパワーが強くなってる。 ‥‥キケンだな）
English: (He's chucking those seeds harder than ever! I better be careful...)

Japanese: パシッと決めていこう！ なるほどくん！
English: Come on, Nick! Nail him!

Japanese: それは、さっきも 聞きましたが‥‥
English: The court has already heard that testimony, Mr. Kudo...

Japanese: わかっとる！　コイツは、 ハナシの“まくら”だ！
English: I know that! I was just setting the mood.

Japanese: このあとのサスペンスを 盛り上げるためのテクニックだよ！
English: How else am I supposed to build up the suspense, hmm!?

Japanese: さ、さすぺんす‥‥
English: The suspense?

Japanese: なんだったら、 もっと引っぱるか？
English: Isn't it enough? Should I work the audience a bit more?

Japanese: いやいやいや！ アッサリ風味でおねがいします！
English: No, no! Please continue, Mr. Kudo. As quickly as possible!

Japanese: そう‥‥あのワカゾウが 机に、バッタリ行ったとき‥‥
English: Alright. Now where was I...? ...Ah yes. The young boy slumped over the table, and...

Japanese: 花ビン‥‥ですか？
English: A vase, you say?

Japanese: あのメシ屋の机の上には、 花ビンが置かれとるのだ。
English: Yes. There are vases on all the tables in that place.

Japanese: “倒してください”と言わんばかり の、きわめて不安定な花ビンが！
English: They're accidents waiting to happen! They're practically begging to be knocked over!

Japanese: （そういえば、たしかに‥‥）
English: (Well, he's right about there being vases. I do remember seeing them, too...)

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくがランチを食べた テーブルにも、花ビンがあった）
English: (There was a vase on the table when I ate lunch there.)

Japanese: それが倒れるところを 見たわけですか？
English: And you saw the moment when the victim actually knocked over the vase?

Japanese: まあ‥‥ややアヤフヤ、かつ ややアイマイで、ややオボロゲ‥‥
English: Well... it's hard to say... It's a bit unclear... How do you really define...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいです。
English: OK, I get it.

Japanese: とにかく、花ビンがコワれた音と 光景は、目に焼きついとる！
English: I heard it break! And the sight of it is burned into my memory as clear as day!

Japanese: 水がこぼれたわけですか。
English: Soaked in water, you mean?

Japanese: そうだ。ワシのヒザにも かかった。
English: Yes. It splashed me on the knee as well.

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、被害者の向かい側の テーブルに座っていたんですよね？
English: You said you were sitting at a table across the room from the victim, correct?

Japanese: それなのに‥‥ 水がかかったんですか？
English: And yet, the water still managed to splash onto you?

Japanese: だって、冷たかったもんね！ ヤツが倒れたとき。
English: It was cold! I clearly remember it splashing me!

Japanese: （そんなにも、とび散るものか？ 花ビンの水なんて‥‥）
English: (Could the water really make it all the way across to the other side of the room...?)

Japanese: 被害者のテーブルの花ビンが倒れて 割れたコト、ですか？
English: You mean, the vase on the victim's table falling upside-down and breaking?

Japanese: 花ビンが“ひっくり返った”コトと 証言が“ひっくり返る”コト‥‥
English: The vase turned upside-down, and my testimony's turning this case upside-down...

Japanese: ちょっとシャレがきいとるだろ！ そこを聞いていただきたかった！
English: It's a joke! I just wanted everyone to hear it!

Japanese: いかがか！　大将！
English: What do you think, Captain!?

Japanese: ‥‥いいんじゃねえか。 どうでも。
English: ...I'm impressed. By your ability to waste time.

Japanese: （さっきから、まったくクチを 挟まないな、ゴドー検事‥‥）
English: (Godot hasn't raised any objections for a while now...)

Japanese: セコいネタかもしれんが、 これが勝負だ、ワカゾウ！
English: So, you young show-off! With that scrap of information, I'm throwing down the gauntlet!

Japanese: これでワシが負けたら、 いさぎよくシャッポをぬぐワイ！
English: If I lose this one, I'll take it like a man and admit defeat!

Japanese: 五十嵐さん。 ‥‥こいつは、現場の写真です。
English: Mr. Kudo. This is a photograph of the crime scene.

Japanese: む‥‥そ、それがどうした！
English: Hmph! So what?

Japanese: 被害者のテーブルを よく見てください。
English: Look carefully at the table.

Japanese: 花ビンなら‥‥ ちゃんと、ありますよ。
English: The vase is there... intact!

Japanese: あれ‥‥‥
English: Huh?

Japanese: どうなんですか？　おとうさん。
English: Lost your tongue, granddad?

Japanese: アンタに“おとうさん” 呼ばわりされたくないッ！
English: I'm no granddad of yours, hop scotch!

Japanese: いてててててててて！
English: Ow! Oooow!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、けっこうです！
English: Enough!

Japanese: これ以上、法廷にマメを まかれては、後かたづけがタイヘン
English: If you throw any more seeds in this courtroom, the cleaners will be here all night.

Japanese: あああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: 今度は、なんですか？
English: What is it now?

Japanese: ワシ、思い出したワ！
English: I just remembered something!

Japanese: な、ナニを‥‥？
English: Y-Yes...? Go on...

Japanese: 割れた花ビン‥‥ あれ、ワシのテーブルだったワ！
English: The broken vase... Ha ha! It was on my table!

Japanese: はい‥‥？
English: Wha...?

Japanese: いや、その。
English: Ah, well... You see...

Japanese: あのワカゾウ、いきなり ぶっ倒れたもんだから‥‥、
English: It startled me when that young lad collapsed.

Japanese: ワシも思わず、 立ち上がっちゃったワケだ。
English: So I stood up.

Japanese: そのとき、割れたんだっけ。 ワシの席の花ビンが！
English: That must've been when it fell over. The vase on my table, I mean.

Japanese: あ‥‥“あなたの席の”‥‥？
English: The vase on your table?

Japanese: うはは。 ‥‥そうみたいだの。
English: Ah ha ha! Yes, it was on my table!

Japanese: それで、ワシのマタグラが しっぽり、ぬれそぼったワケだ。
English: And that's how my groin came to be completely soaked.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 証人。ごくろうさまでした。
English: *sigh* Thank you, Mr. Kudo. You've certainly earned your kudos for today.

Japanese: あの。チョットうかがいたい。
English: Er, I'd like to ask a question now.

Japanese: ケッキョク、ワシはその‥‥ 役に立ったのだろうか。
English: Have I, er... Have I been any use at all?

Japanese: 自分のムネに聞きなさい。
English: Perhaps that's something you should reflect on yourself, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: ぐわわああああっ！
English: Aaaargh!

Japanese: ままま、待っていただきたい！ そ、そういうことであれば、その。
English: WAIT! WAIT A MINUTE! If that's the case, there's more! I've got more to say!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そうだ！ なんなら、あの話を‥‥
English: Oh, yes! I remember something else...

Japanese: 法廷係官！ この証人をつまみ出しなさい。
English: Bailiff! Escort the witness out of the courtroom!

Japanese: 待ってくだされ！ ワシの話を聞いてくれええええ！
English: WAAAIT! LISTEN TO MEEEEEE!

Japanese: さて‥‥。最後によけいな 回り道をしてしまいましたが‥‥
English: Well! We seem to have been considerably sidetracked.

Japanese: とにかく、この場で判決を 下すことは、不可能です。
English: And I am still not in a position to deliver a verdict.

Japanese: 毒を入れたウエイトレスは、 被告人だと特定できませんでした。
English: The defendant has not been positively identified as the waitress in question.

Japanese: それに‥‥大きなムジュン点が、 ２つあります。
English: Additionally, there are two disparities in the testimony we have heard thus far.

Japanese: 左手でコーヒーを飲んだ被害者と、 コーヒーカップに残った跡。
English: The mark on the coffee cup that the victim supposedly drank from with his left hand.

Japanese: そして、聞こえないはずの左耳に つけられた、イヤホンの問題。
English: And the earpiece which was inserted into his left ear, out of which he couldn't hear.

Japanese: かー、やるね。なるほどくん！
English: Wow, Nick! You did it!

Japanese: 検察側・弁護側に、 さらなる調査を命じます。
English: I therefore require both the defense and the prosecution to further investigate the facts.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: では本日は、これにて閉廷！ ‥‥の前に。
English: There is one more thing before I call today's session to an end.

Japanese: ま、まだナニか あるんですか？
English: O-One more thing, Your Honor?

Japanese: さきほどの証人が‥‥、
English: The witness we just heard from...

Japanese: 『どうしても自分の証言を 役に立ててほしい』と。
English: He is most insistent that his testimony should be of use.

Japanese: このように、証言書を まとめてくれました。
English: So he's summarized it accordingly into this statement.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Um... OK...

Japanese: よろしかったら、 みなさんでどうぞ。
English: You may each have a copy of it, if you wish.

Japanese: 好きにするがいいぜ。 ‥‥検察側には必要ねえ。
English: Whatever. The prosecution doesn't need props like that.

Japanese: ゴドー検事さん、怒ってるね。
English: Godot's really mad, huh?

Japanese: ま。ムリもないんじゃないかな。
English: Yeah. I would be, too.

Japanese: じゃあ。弁護人。
English: Very well.

Japanese: これ、あげます。ゴドー検事と 私のぶんと、３枚ありますから。
English: Here you are then, Mr. Wright. There are 3 copies. My own, yours, and Mr. Godot's.

Japanese: え。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 証拠品《五十嵐の証言書》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Victor's Testimony added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （“ごめんなさい”って‥‥ 小学生の反省文かよ）
English: ("I'm sorry"? This isn't a piece of testimony! More like a five year old's apology...)

Japanese: どうするの？　こんなの ‥‥３枚も。
English: What the heck are we supposed to do with 3 copies...?

Japanese: それでは、とにかく‥‥ 本日は、これにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.
